message:
feat(package_manager): validate extension file types to reject non-manifest sources

Add is_supported_extension_file() predicate that accepts only recognized
extension manifest formats: extension.json, *.native.json, and *.wasm.
This prevents the package manager from accidentally loading arbitrary
files (e.g. .js scripts, README files) as extensions when they appear
in extension directories or are referenced in package.json#pi.extensions.

Apply the validation at three ingestion points:
1. Direct file path resolution in resolve_local_extension_source() —
   warns and skips unsupported files when a source points to a single file
2. collect_files_from_paths() when resource type is Extensions — filters
   out unsupported entries from explicit path lists
3. resolve_extension_entries() for package.json#pi.extensions arrays —
   warns and skips entries that resolve to unsupported file types

Update all tests to use .native.json extension files instead of .js,
matching the new validation: test fixtures now write {} to .native.json
files, and assertions reference the updated paths. This ensures the
test suite exercises the validated path and catches regressions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
